name: Osie Johnson
description: American jazz drummer, arranger and singer (1923–1966)
abstract: James "Osie" Johnson (January 11, 1923, in Washington, D.C. – February 10, 1966, in New York City) was a jazz drummer, arranger and singer.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: And that’s what we tried to do, the three of us, Hank Jones, Barry Galbraith, Osie Johnson and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Mona Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: Osie Johnson the drummer, Barry Galbraith, the guitar player, Hank Jones the piano player, and Milton, they would work, they would do at least three sessions a day... They were called the New York, the New York Rhythm Section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mona Hinton interviewed by Milt Fillius and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: He did a recording called Jazz in the Space Age. That was with me and David Young and Al Kiger, because he was using the drummer who used to play with Milt Hinton – Osie Davis – I mean Osie Johnson on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: I had a lot of different drummers. Osie Johnson, Gus Johnson, Eddie Shaughnessy, Joe Morello. And I worked Birdland and The Bohemia...
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: I had Osie Johnson, a great drummer, John Drew the bass player that was with me when I © Fillius Jazz Archive -9- was with Gene Krupa.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: That was the first time meeting him. ... He was Osie Johnson, coming to play with the band, and I didn't know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
